articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- 82% of landlords fear the Renters’ Rights Act could negatively impact their business, with 44% feeling very concerned.  
- 75% of landlords are deterred from expanding their portfolios, and nearly half plan to sell or downsize in the coming months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are urged to boost EPC ratings to attract tenants, with a looming deadline for minimum C ratings by 2030.  
- Increasing energy efficiency is crucial as tenants seek cost savings amid rising energy prices exacerbated by the Iran War.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe that planning reform will benefit their business.  
- Landbay's report highlights a significant lack of confidence in the effectiveness of proposed planning changes.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in properties originate from external sources, highlighting the importance for landlords to maintain outside pipework.  
- Neglecting external maintenance could lead to costly repairs, potentially costing landlords thousands in damages.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective from May 1, 2026, will transform the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales by enhancing tenant protections while imposing new obligations on landlords.  
- Landlords are advised to prepare early, with top tips circulating to ensure compliance with upcoming regulations before the deadline approaches.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Majority Unprepared:** 84% of landlords lack readiness for the upcoming Renters’ Rights Act set to launch on May 1.  
- **Compliance Risk:** 58% of landlords are at high risk of non-compliance with the new regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are prioritizing portfolio restructuring and financing adjustments over expansion in response to slowed rental growth.  
- The ONS report highlights the necessity for landlords to align their portfolios with shifting rental demand trends.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- The findings highlight growing concerns about the accessibility of home ownership for new buyers amid increasing competition from investors.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to acknowledge the vital role of the private rented sector (PRS) and develop a formal strategy to support it.  
- The call comes amid concerns over a declining number of landlords, which could impact the availability of rental properties in Scotland.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting May, HMRC's new tax advisor registration requirements could hit conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams with penalties of up to £10,000 for non-compliance.  
- Landlords must ensure their advisors are registered to avoid hefty fines and ensure compliance with the latest tax regulations.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A local council asserts that new licensing fees for landlords won't drive up rental prices.  
- Landlords face a £694 fee per property with no plan for cost recovery, raising concerns about financial burdens.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- The Labour Party, represented by Matthew Pennycook, has officially stated that it will not introduce rent controls.  
- This decision underscores Labour's focus on other housing strategies rather than imposing restrictions on rental prices.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to curb the “HMO landlord free for all” by implementing stricter regulations.  
- New detailed guidelines for Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) are in development to enhance oversight.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign kicks off to educate tenants on leveraging their powers under the Renters Rights Act.  
- The initiative aims to empower renters with knowledge to help them navigate their rights and improve their housing situations.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, aligning with predictions made by property expert Paul Shamplina.  
- Landlords are increasingly exercising their rights to evict tenants under the new legislation, raising concerns about housing stability for renters.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are on the rise as landlords adjust prices in anticipation of the new Renters Rights Act, according to data from Hamptons.  
- The upcoming legislation is prompting landlords to reassess rental strategies amidst changing regulations in the UK housing market.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act threatens to eliminate hobbyist landlords as it establishes stricter regulations in the rental market.  
- Experts argue that these changes will significantly reshape the landscape of property rental, leading to reduced availability of rental properties.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly turning to property investment, navigating challenges like regulatory hurdles and heightened taxes.  
- This emerging trend reflects a shift in investment strategies among younger generations seeking stability in real estate.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing challenges with many needing to reduce asking prices, deal with failed sales, or pull their properties from the market, according to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson.  
- The current housing market is proving tough as sellers struggle to attract buyers in an increasingly competitive environment.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying its examination of property valuations to ensure accurate Inheritance Tax assessments, leading to a rise in referrals to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This increased scrutiny is contributing to a boost in Inheritance Tax revenue for the UK government.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria, features an unexpected surprise in its listing: a sleeping tenant caught on camera.
- The unusual photos have sparked attention in the property market, raising eyebrows over the £135k asking price.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Conveyancing Task Force labels referral fees as a "hidden tax" that unnecessarily inflates costs for homebuyers.  
- These fees are described as an “unseen surcharge,” contributing to financial strain in the property buying process.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond expands its operations into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group managing its West of Scotland portfolio.
- David Alexander emphasizes the strategic growth of Lomond in the UK property market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue due to unexpected geopolitical challenges affecting buyer activity.  
- CEO Guy Gittins attributes the decline to lower-than-anticipated demand in the property market.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Rising inflation is largely driven by escalating fuel and transport costs due to the Middle East conflict, with broader economic impacts anticipated.
- Experts warn that the current price increases could soon extend to other sectors, affecting overall consumer spending.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Market Exclusion**: A staggering 90% of newly built homes never make it to the open sales market, according to Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.  
- **Industry Insight**: This highlights a significant shift in how new properties are sold, with many bypassing traditional market channels altogether.
